text.title: Idea Scoring Matrix and Common Mistakes
text.summary: 5-criteria weighted scoring matrix for comparing ideas, scoring guide per criterion, and antipatterns to avoid in idea generation sessions.

rule.statement: Score every idea on 5 criteria (Market Size 20%, Personal Fit 25%, Competition 15%, Monetization 20%, Speed to MVP 20%) before selecting any idea to validate.
rule.rationale: Scoring before selecting prevents personal excitement from dominating. 20+ ideas are needed for the matrix to produce signal — below that, there are too few comparison points.

list.item: Market Size (20%): 5 = billion-dollar opportunity, 1 = tiny niche.
Personal Fit (25%): 5 = excited to work on this daily, 1 = dreading the work.
Competition (15%): 5 = blue ocean, 1 = dominated by well-funded giants.
Monetization (20%): 5 = clear revenue path, 1 = unclear how to charge.
Speed to MVP (20%): 5 = 2 weeks, 1 = 1+ year to validate.

rule.statement: Score Personal Fit before viewing any market data; excitement in market context contaminates the honest answer.
rule.rationale: Personal Fit has the highest weight (25%) and is the only criterion an LLM cannot score. If the agent fills it in, the row must be discarded and re-scored by the founder.

antipattern.description: Waiting for the perfect idea before starting research.
antipattern.reason: Inspiration does not precede research; research produces inspiration. Generate 10+ ideas, score them, pick the best one to explore further.

antipattern.description: Running only one or two of the seven frameworks.
antipattern.reason: Different frameworks surface different idea classes. Skipping frameworks locks the output into a single idea type.

antipattern.description: Not documenting every idea, even obviously bad ones.
antipattern.reason: The rejected list compounds over months. Patterns in rejected ideas often reveal the best path forward 6-12 months later.

antipattern.description: Quoting global TAM as Market Size instead of ICP segment size.
antipattern.reason: A $100B global market is irrelevant for a solopreneur product. Constrain Market Size to the actual ICP segment the product can realistically reach.

text.reference: Python script: reads JSON ideas with criterion scores, computes weighted totals, outputs top-10
